publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-09-21 18:13:28 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Correspondence with the American Dairy Association, other agricultural organizations, manufacturers of dairy machinery, commercial laboratories, and other professors of dairy science concerning experiments, studies of cultured cream, butter making, stock judging at Cornell, and the naming of the dairy building after William A. Stocking.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Dairy Industry, Cornell University.
Edward S. Guthrie received his M.S. and Ph.D. from Cornell in 1910 and 1913.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with the American Dairy Association, other agricultural organizations, manufacturers of dairy machinery, commercial laboratories, and other professors of dairy science concerning experiments, studies of cultured cream, butter making, stock judging at Cornell, and the naming of the dairy building after William A. Stocking; also, Professor Guthrie's laboratory notebooks, texts of various broadcasts he gave (1929-1934) for the Cornell Agricultural Hour on stations WEAI and WESG, his articles, some charts and photographs relating to dairy processes, annual department letters (Nos. 1-7, 1949-1954, 1957) sent to alumni, and printed material.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Edward Sewall Guthrie papers, #21-20-800. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Dairy stock.
Cream.
Butter -- Production.
Milk -- Research.
Dairy science.
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences -- : Faculty.

did.unittitle: American Dairy Science Association. Necrology Com., 1946-1947 , and
880 - A Study of the Body of Cultured Cream,
Membership and Subscription B...(rest ripped off)
Report to President of the Cornell Dairy Science Association by the Industrial Relations Committee
Medal - H.P. Hood and Sons, Walter S. Green
Three loose mounted photographs, and some loose papers
Vogt Method of Making Flake Buttermilk
Brochures and Replies - Dairy Depts. and Libraries
Jensen Machinery Company, Inc. material (loose) including brochures and photograph
Notebook containing annual letters to Alumni Dept. of Dairy Industry,
Bundle of envelopes (contain radio broadcasts, re butter, buttermilk, etc.), WEAI, WESG, 1929-1934 , Agr. Coll.
Unlabeled envelope (contains printed papers of U.S. Dept. of Agriculture, Bureau of Dairy Industry; what appear to be mss. of various reports, by Gurthie; some correspondence)
Loose: some papers dealing with milk (published?) by E.S. Gurthie and B.L. Herrington
Envelope of correspondence: American Dairy Science Association, U. of Wisconsin, Michigan State
Loose: "Crowley's Quarterly," (2 issues), and some milk sheets
Loose: Misc. reports, accounts, calculations; several newspapers and clippings; cow accounts or production records(?)
Folder: Time of Lactation, Winter and Summer Feeding, BLH, ESG,
Pamphlet: Appellants Brief, United States Court of Customs and Patent Appeals
Envelope of correspondence with Arthur D. Little, Inc., Research-Engineering-Investigation
Envelope containing mounted photograph. List of signatures clipped to outside
Loose: Milk Flavor Clinic materials (many dupls.); clipping; New York State Association of Milk Sanitarians Newsletter
"Old Ornage Houses, Organe County, New York," Vol. II, by Mildred Parker Seese
Book: Old Organe Houses by Mildred Parker Seese
Four Notebooks labeled E.S. Gurthrie, Cultured Cream
E.S. Guthrie, Stocking Hall - (hard cover notebook)
E.S. Guthrie, Book 2, Cod Liver Oil, etc. - (hard cover notebook)
Butter Research, J. Campbell - (hard cover notebook)
Oxidized Flavors, Mar. 1951 , E.S. Guthrie - (hard cover notebook)
E.S. Guthrie, Stocking Hall, 1955 - (hard cover notebook)
Book Started Feb. 25, 195? , Warren family P17, Cornell P21, Ascorbic Acid, Light, Flavor. Guthrie, Hand, Shep. - (hard cover notebook)
E.S. Guthrie, Stocking, ESG and BLH, 1954 - (hard cover notebook)
Oxidized Flavors, 1935 , E.S. Guthrie, Tyler and Sheperd
Guthrie and Scheib, 1935 , Butter from Isolated Bacteria, E.S. Guthrie
Deaeration vs. no deaeration, E.S. Guthrie, Daylight Studies, Cultured Cream, Sour Cream, last part of book
Reports of scans of products received for any criticism, E.S. Guthrie
Tied bundle of notes, no title
Sour Cream IIIV, Sour Cream, Aug. 28, 1933 , Dr. E.S. Guthrie, H. Vorperian (?)
I, Sour Cream Theory of Churning II
Primary Flavors of Milk, E.S. Guthrie
Bankhead Jones Project - Scores of Butter Samples by E.S. Guthrie
Corrosin - Taylor Instrument Co., Ames St. Cor. Albert Ave., Rochester, E.S. Guthrie
Letters recommending the Dairy Building be named Stocking Hall,
Three large felt banners, two 1923 , one 1922 , for judging at exposition of dairy products
Large drawing of residence of Geo. Gouge Campbell Hall, Hamptonburg Town, Organe Co., N.Y.
Photograph approx 18" by 8" of creamery at Newfield
Evelope containing: positive and negative photograph of heard of cows; photos of classes; pos. and neg. photograph of cow and calf; pos. and neg. photograph - milking scene, Argentina
Envelope containing mounted photograph of Cornell Conference, Delhi Tech. Dairy Industry Seniors
Envelope (small) of various slides, and pos. and neg. photographs, such as first butter factory, Campbell Hall, P.O., etc.
Two photographs of wooden butter tubs
Mounted photo.of various professors (?) taken in Robert's Hall around
Letter to Guthrie from Mrs. William A. Stocking,
Drawings on a sort of cloth-paper of milk equipment (?)
"The Cornell Countryman," Vol. XLV, Mar. 1948 , No. 6
Photogrpahs (glossy prints) of Pine Grove Farm
Four brochures of first butter factory
Commencement Program for Ohio State University, Class of
Excerpts from New York Produce Review and American Creamery
Storage Milk, Cream and whole milk powder (small notebook)
Accuracy of the Vapor-Odor Method of Grading Milk (small notebook)
Book I, 1961 , Purchased Cultured Cream, E.S. Guthrie, Stocking Hall (small notebook)
